rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and data types.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees; avoid implementation-specific subcomponents in type contracts.
generic_rules: Declare constants and equations in AGREE annexes using valid AADL data types and visible feature names.
Ensure that range assumptions and guarantees are expressed with concrete bounds or named constants.
Write deterministic dependency guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs without using implementation-only paths.
Avoid encoding AADL properties such as timing or resource constraints directly in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid identifiers present in the AADL model scope.
Boolean expressions in AGREE should match the actual data types of the referenced ports or variables.
Use `eq` for defining helper variables and equations in AGREE contracts.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are explicitly visible.
Do not assume that AADL property names like Period or Deadline are automatically available in AGREE scope.
Do not convert Boolean or opaque data ports into integer comparisons unless the model explicitly defines numeric representations.
Do not generate range assumptions or guarantees without concrete bounds or visible scalar relations.
irrelevant_topics: AADL model fusion and component implementation insertion locations
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
Model analyst extraction of component structure
